preamble: The elision of [s] is a major feature of Romance phonology. It often follows a debuccalization path ([s] > [h] > [∅]), particularly in coda positions. In French, this historical loss is often marked by a circumflex accent on the preceding vowel.

examples.note: Loss of s before a consonant.
Dialectal elision of final and syllable-coda s.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
